name: Credential Leakage via Public GitHub Repository (T1552.004)

pg-api-keys-rotated.description: All leaked API keys and tokens rotated

pg-rdp-sessions-cleared.description: RDP cached credentials cleared and sessions terminated

pg-kerberos-tickets-purged.description: Kerberos tickets purged on all affected systems

pg-oauth-tokens-revoked.description: OAuth/SAML tokens revoked for affected accounts

pg-bsi-notified.description: BSI (Bundesamt für Sicherheit in der Informationstechnik) notified

pg-secret-scanning-enabled.description: Secret scanning enabled on all repositories

proc-root.name: Credential Leakage via Public GitHub Response
proc-root.notes: End-to-end response for credentials leaked to a public GitHub repository. Covers detection, immediate credential rotation, session termination (including RDP cached credentials), regulatory notification (LDI, BSI, LKA/BKA), and long-term hardening (gMSA migration, secret scanning).

proc-detect.name: Detection
proc-detect.notes: Detect credential leakage via GitHub secret scanning alerts, external threat intelligence feeds, or third-party notification (e.g., security researcher, automated scanners like truffleHog/GitLeaks).

proc-detect-github-alert.name: GitHub Secret Scanning Alert
proc-detect-github-alert.notes: GitHub Advanced Security secret scanning or push protection triggers alert on committed credentials (API keys, connection strings, private keys, passwords in config files).

proc-detect-external-notify.name: External Notification / Threat Intel
proc-detect-external-notify.notes: Credential leakage reported by external party (security researcher, CERT, partner notification) or discovered via dark web monitoring.

proc-analysis.name: Analysis & Impact Assessment
proc-analysis.notes: Determine scope of leaked credentials: identify all secrets in commit history, determine exposure window, check for unauthorized access using leaked credentials, assess whether customer/personal data is affected (triggers GDPR Art. 33/34 obligations).

proc-scan-git-history.name: Scan Full Git History for Secrets
proc-scan-git-history.notes: Run truffleHog, GitLeaks, or equivalent across full commit history of affected repository. Include all branches, tags, and force-pushed commits. Document all unique credentials found with commit timestamps.

proc-check-unauthorized-access.name: Check for Unauthorized Access
proc-check-unauthorized-access.notes: Review authentication logs for all leaked credentials since exposure window start. Check VPN logs, cloud console access, API call history, RDP/SSH session logs for anomalous usage from unknown sources.

proc-assess-data-impact.name: Assess Customer Data Impact (GDPR)
proc-assess-data-impact.notes: Determine if leaked credentials could access personal data (Art. 4 GDPR). If customer data is potentially affected, this triggers mandatory notification to supervisory authority (LDI) within 72 hours per Art. 33 GDPR and potentially data subjects per Art. 34.

proc-contain.name: Containment - Credential Rotation & Session Termination
proc-contain.notes: Immediately rotate ALL leaked credentials. Terminate all active sessions for affected accounts including RDP sessions (to prevent cached credential reuse), purge Kerberos tickets, revoke OAuth/SAML tokens. Remove or make repository private.

proc-rotate-passwords.name: Rotate All Leaked Passwords
proc-rotate-passwords.notes: Immediately reset passwords for all accounts whose credentials appeared in the repository. Include AD accounts, database accounts, application accounts. Force password change on next logon for interactive accounts.

proc-rotate-api-keys.name: Rotate API Keys & Service Tokens
proc-rotate-api-keys.notes: Regenerate all leaked API keys, connection strings, SAS tokens, and cloud service credentials. Update all dependent applications and CI/CD pipelines with new credentials stored in proper secret management (Azure Key Vault, HashiCorp Vault).

proc-revoke-certs.name: Revoke Leaked Certificates & Private Keys
proc-revoke-certs.notes: Revoke any TLS/SSL certificates whose private keys were exposed. Issue replacement certificates. Update services with new certificates.

proc-terminate-sessions.name: Terminate All Active Sessions
proc-terminate-sessions.notes: Critical: Terminate ALL active sessions for affected accounts to prevent use of cached credentials. This includes: (1) Terminate RDP sessions and clear cached RDP credentials (cmdkey /delete, reg delete HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Terminal Server Client), (2) Purge Kerberos tickets (klist purge) on all systems where affected accounts logged in, (3) Revoke OAuth/SAML refresh tokens, (4) Invalidate SSO sessions, (5) Force logoff from all workstations via GPO or scripted logoff.

proc-remove-repo.name: Remove or Privatize Repository
proc-remove-repo.notes: Immediately make the public repository private or delete it. Note: GitHub caches and forks may retain the data. Contact GitHub support for DMCA-based removal of cached content if needed. Scrub secrets from git history using BFG Repo-Cleaner or git filter-repo.

proc-notify-authorities.name: Regulatory & Law Enforcement Notification
proc-notify-authorities.notes: If customer personal data is potentially affected, notify German authorities per legal requirements: LDI (Landesbeauftragte für Datenschutz und Informationsfreiheit) within 72h per GDPR Art. 33, BSI (Bundesamt für Sicherheit in der Informationstechnik) per BSI-Gesetz §8b if critical infrastructure, LKA/BKA (Landeskriminalamt/Bundeskriminalamt) for criminal prosecution of unauthorized access.

proc-notify-ldi.name: Notify LDI (Data Protection Authority)
proc-notify-ldi.notes: File data breach notification with the responsible Landesbeauftragte für Datenschutz und Informationsfreiheit within 72 hours of becoming aware (GDPR Art. 33). Include: nature of breach, categories/number of affected data subjects, likely consequences, measures taken/proposed. If customer data subjects face high risk, notify them directly per Art. 34.

proc-notify-bsi.name: Notify BSI
proc-notify-bsi.notes: Report incident to BSI (Bundesamt für Sicherheit in der Informationstechnik) via their incident reporting portal. Mandatory for KRITIS operators per BSI-Gesetz §8b. Recommended for all organizations to contribute to national threat picture. BSI may provide technical assistance and IOC sharing.

proc-notify-lka.name: Notify LKA/BKA (Law Enforcement)
proc-notify-lka.notes: File criminal complaint (Strafanzeige) with LKA (Landeskriminalamt) Cybercrime division or BKA (Bundeskriminalamt) if cross-border. Relevant offenses: §202a StGB (Ausspähen von Daten), §202b StGB (Abfangen von Daten), §303a/b StGB (Datenveränderung/Computersabotage). Preserve all evidence for prosecution.

proc-eradicate.name: Eradication
proc-eradicate.notes: Verify no unauthorized access occurred during exposure window. If access confirmed, perform full forensic investigation of affected systems. Clean git history of all secrets.

proc-clean-git-history.name: Scrub Secrets from Git History
proc-clean-git-history.notes: Use BFG Repo-Cleaner or git filter-repo to remove secrets from all commits in history. Force-push cleaned history. Invalidate all GitHub caches. Notify fork owners.

proc-forensic-compromised-systems.name: Forensic Analysis of Accessed Systems
proc-forensic-compromised-systems.notes: If unauthorized access was confirmed during the exposure window, perform full forensic analysis of affected systems. Check for backdoors, persistence mechanisms, data exfiltration indicators.

proc-harden.name: Hardening & Rework
proc-harden.notes: Implement long-term fixes: migrate technical/service accounts from static passwords to gMSA (Group Managed Service Accounts), implement secret management solution, enable GitHub secret scanning and push protection, enforce pre-commit hooks for secret detection.

proc-migrate-gmsa.name: Migrate Service Accounts to gMSA
proc-migrate-gmsa.notes: Identify all technical/service accounts that were using static passwords (especially those leaked). Migrate to Group Managed Service Accounts (gMSA) where AD automatically rotates 240-character passwords every 30 days. For non-Windows services, implement equivalent automated credential rotation via vault integration. This eliminates the root cause of static credential exposure.

proc-enable-secret-scanning.name: Enable Secret Scanning & Push Protection
proc-enable-secret-scanning.notes: Enable GitHub Advanced Security secret scanning on all organization repositories. Enable push protection to block commits containing secrets before they reach the remote. Configure pre-commit hooks (detect-secrets, gitleaks) in CI/CD pipelines.

proc-implement-vault.name: Implement Secret Management Solution
proc-implement-vault.notes: Deploy centralized secret management (Azure Key Vault, HashiCorp Vault, AWS Secrets Manager). Migrate all application secrets from code/config files to vault. Implement dynamic secrets where possible. Enforce secret access auditing.

proc-lessons.name: Lessons Learned
proc-lessons.notes: Post-incident review: assess developer security awareness gaps, secret management maturity, code review processes, CI/CD pipeline security controls, and regulatory notification process efficiency.

ref-002.name: GDPR Art. 33/34 - Data Breach Notification Requirements

bo-001.scope: All credentials and secrets used across the organization
bo-001.purpose: Ensure no unauthorized access is possible via leaked credentials
bo-001.context: Leaked credentials provide direct unauthorized access; speed of rotation determines exposure window

bo-002.scope: GDPR compliance and regulatory obligations in Germany
bo-002.purpose: Meet all legal notification obligations within mandated timeframes
bo-002.context: Failure to notify LDI within 72h can result in significant GDPR fines (up to 4% annual turnover); criminal reporting enables prosecution

bo-003.scope: Developer workflows and CI/CD pipeline security
bo-003.purpose: Prevent future credential leakage through technical controls and process improvements
bo-003.context: Root cause is typically lack of secret management tooling and developer awareness; gMSA eliminates static service account passwords entirely

smg-001.context: Time between leak detection and credential rotation is the critical exposure window
smg-001.scope: All leaked credentials identified in the repository
smg-001.purpose: Minimize the window of opportunity for attackers to use leaked credentials
smg-001.focus: Time-to-rotate for all identified leaked credentials
smg-001.criteria: All leaked credentials rotated within 1 hour of detection; all sessions terminated within 2 hours

smg-002.context: GDPR mandates 72-hour notification window; German law enforcement requires timely reporting for effective prosecution
smg-002.scope: All regulatory notification obligations triggered by the incident
smg-002.purpose: Ensure all authority notifications are filed within legal deadlines
smg-002.focus: Notification completeness and timeliness for LDI, BSI, and LKA/BKA
smg-002.criteria: LDI notified within 72h; BSI notified within 24h; LKA contacted within 7 days

sm-001.description: Time elapsed from leak detection to complete credential rotation and session termination
sm-001.goal: Minimize attacker opportunity window by achieving rapid credential invalidation
sm-001.method: Track timestamps from initial alert to last credential rotated and last session terminated
sm-001.interpretation: Under 1 hour is excellent; 1-4 hours is acceptable; over 4 hours indicates process failures
sm-001.reporting: Post-incident report; included in KPI dashboard for security operations

sm-002.description: Percentage of mandatory authority notifications filed within legal deadline
sm-002.goal: Achieve full regulatory compliance and avoid penalties
sm-002.method: Track notification filing timestamps against legal deadlines for each authority
sm-002.interpretation: 100% is required; anything below indicates compliance failure with potential legal consequences
sm-002.reporting: Immediate escalation if deadline at risk; included in post-incident compliance report

bm-001.description: Elapsed minutes from leak detection to completion of all credential rotations and session terminations

bm-002.description: Count of authority notifications filed within their respective legal deadlines

bm-003.description: Total count of mandatory authority notifications required for this incident
